data.title: Bitcoin price forecasts eye $110K target as $4.9T options expiry arrives
data.description:
Key points:

  • Bitcoin fails to close above $117,200, opening the door to support retests.

  • A giant $4.9 trillion options expiry event adds further friction for Bitcoin bulls on Friday.

  • Order-book liquidity shows bids massing at $110,000 and above, creating a “magnet” for price.

Bitcoin missed a key daily close into Friday as traders expected short-term BTC price losses.

Bitcoin adds headwinds after daily close miss

Data from Cointelegraph Markets Pro and TradingView showed that narrowly avoided a daily close above $117,200.

This had been one of the key lines in the sand on short timeframes — a reclaim would allow price to revisit $120,000, analysis said.

Rekt Capital
@rektcapital

#BTC

Bitcoin is on the cusp of printing a Daily Close inside the Range to kickstart the reclaim process

Bitcoin is one Daily Close above ~$117.2k away from preparing for a revisit of ~$120k+$BTC #Crypto #Bitcoin https://t.co/AMROY2kutn pic.twitter.com/EFi4CJTpOB

Sep 18, 2025

“Once we gain this level the way to $120K is open in my opinion,” popular trader Crypto Caesar wrote Thursday in part of an X post on the topic alongside an explanatory chart. 

“However: Last time we rejected this level and came all the way back to the light blue zone.”

Crypto investor and entrepreneur Ted Pillows predicted downward BTC price pressure continuing through the week’s options expiry event.

“$BTC failed to reclaim the $117,200 level again. Today, $4.9 trillion in US stock futures and options will expire,” he told X followers. 

“Historically, this has resulted in downside volatility and consolidation in the stock market. And because the crypto market follows US stocks, volatility will shift into Bitcoin and alts too. Be prepared.”

Trader bids create $113,000 BTC price “magnet”

Other arguments in favor of lower levels focused on exchange order-book liquidity.

Updating X followers, trading resource TheKingfisher noted that most liquidity now lies below the price. The area between $110,000 and $113,000 was of particular interest.

TheKingfisher
@kingfisher_btc

Here's the deal, most traders are still guessing. But look at this Kingfisher heatmap for $BTC.

See those dense zones around 110k and 113k? Those are long liq zones. Price is magnets to these levels, flushing out overleveraged positions.

Above current price, there are thin,… pic.twitter.com/JDYSeUFDbg

Sep 19, 2025

Onchain analytics platform Glassnode agreed, noting shifts in order-book composition after Wednesday’s Federal Reserve meeting.

There, the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) lowered interest rates for the first time in 2025, leading to new all-time highs for both gold and US stock markets.

“Post FOMC, we can see that $BTC shorts at 117k were taken out, and long liquidations are appearing at 112.7k,” Glassnode summarized.

This article does not contain investment advice or recommendations. Every investment and trading move involves risk, and readers should conduct their own research when making a decision.

data.title: China's market regulator probes Kuaishou's e-commerce unit
data.description:
China's market regulator launched an investigation into Kauigou, an e-commerce unit of live-streaming service provider Kuaishou Technology , over suspected violations of China's e-commerce law, it said on Friday.

The live-streaming e-commerce industry is "plagued by illegal and irregular occurrences such as false marketing and counterfeit products despite repeated bans," the State Administration for Market Regulation said in a statement.

The investigation is aimed at protecting consumers and small and medium-sized businesses, it added.

Kuaigou will "actively cooperate with the regulatory authorities in the investigation and take this opportunity to improve our compliance level," the company said in a statement released later on Friday via its social media account.

data.title: Energy companies down as demand fears overshadow US Fed rate cut
data.description:
** S&P 500 energy index down 0.8% as oil prices edge lower

** Brent crude down ~0.5% at $67.13/barrel and U.S. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude futures down 0.6% at $63.16/barrel

data.title: Wall St ticks higher with indexes on track for weekly gains; FedEx jumps
data.description:
Key points:
  • Indexes up: Dow 0.07%, S&P 500 0.21%, Nasdaq 0.44%
  • FedEx results top targets on cost-cutting, shares jump
  • Lennar falls after quarterly profit declines
  • Trump, Xi hold first call since June

By Purvi Agarwal and Sukriti Gupta

Wall Street's main indexes nudged higher on Friday, with the Nasdaq continuing the previous session's rally to hit an intraday record, while FedEx rose as the parcel delivery firm posted upbeat quarterly results.

FedEx gained 1.7% after reporting quarterly profit and revenue above analyst estimates on Thursday, as cost-cutting and strength in domestic deliveries helped offset weaker international volumes.

Apple gained 1.3% following a price target raise from J.P. Morgan, while Tesla was up 2.6% after a report of Baird's upgrade on the stock to "outperform" from "neutral".

Their gains boosted the S&P 500 tech and consumer discretionary sectors , up 0.5% and 0.8% respectively.

Conversely, financials and energy companies lost 0.3% and 0.9% respectively.

"We're still just calming from the Fed decision, what the expectation for monetary policy and the labor outlook is and how they're going to coexist," said Kevin Gordon, senior investment strategist at Charles Schwab.

"It's probably time for the market to sit back and reassess after a really strong rally."

At 10:03 a.m. ET, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 31.68 points, or 0.07%, to 46,174.10, the S&P 500 gained 13.74 points, or 0.21%, to 6,645.76 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 98.63 points, or 0.44%, to 22,569.36.

The small-cap Russell 2000 index was down 0.1% after briefly hitting an intraday record high on the day. It notched a record close on Thursday, its first since November 2021.

The three indexes were set for weekly gains, wrapping up the week with the Fed's first rate cut of 2025 and indications of further monetary policy easing. Revived optimism around AI-linked stock trading also added to the rise.

Meanwhile, U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping spoke by phone, Chinese state media and a U.S. official said, as they seek to help keep TikTok online in the U.S. and ease tensions between two superpowers locked in a standoff over trade.

"To the extent it's (the call) wrapped into some broader discussion around trade talks and tariffs ... that's probably where the more important factor is," Gordon added.

Analysts expect some volatility from "triple witching", where options and futures linked to stock indexes and individual stocks are set to expire on the third Friday of the last month of the quarter.

Wall Street's three main indexes are in positive territory so far in September - a month deemed historically bad for U.S. equities. The benchmark S&P 500 has shed 1.4% on average in the month since 2000, according to data compiled by LSEG.

Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari on Friday said he feels that quarter-point sized rate cuts at each of the Fed's final two meetings of the year will be appropriate.

San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly is set to speak later in the day.

In other stocks, Lennar fell 2.5% after the homebuilder reported a lower third-quarter profit and forecast fourth-quarter home deliveries below estimates.

Declining issues outnumbered advancers by a 1.32-to-1 ratio on the NYSE and by a 1.28-to-1 ratio on the Nasdaq.

The S&P 500 posted 15 new 52-week highs and seven new lows, while the Nasdaq Composite recorded 90 new highs and 21 new lows.

Fathom Holdings Shares Fall on Public Offering Price
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Fathom Holdings shares fell after the company priced its underwritten public offering.

The stock slid 16% to $2.02. Shares are still up 33% this year.

The Cary, N.C., real-estate services platform is offering three million shares at $2 apiece. Fathom expects to receive $5.4 million in net proceeds, which it plans to use for general purposes.

Fathom also granted the underwriter of the offering a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 15% of shares.

The offering is set to close on Sept. 22.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Base Metals Mixed But Firmer U.S. Dollar Pressures Prices — Market Talk
data.description:
1029 ET - Base metal prices are mixed in afternoon trade, with a firmer U.S. dollar weighing on the complex. Copper futures on the London Metal Exchange are up 0.3% to $9,975 a metric ton, while aluminum falls 1.3% to $2,671.50 a ton, with market watchers saying any benefit from U.S. rate cuts might be delayed until real economic activity picks up. "The picture on the aluminum market remains unclear," says Commerzbank's Thu Lan Nguyen. "After the metal received a boost last week from a sharp rise in cancelled warrants on the LME, inventories recently rose again more strongly, dampening hopes of a tightening market." In copper news, Nguyen points out that if the International Copper Study Group reveals next week that the copper market was notably oversupplied in the first seven months of 2025, it could delay a near-term recovery in prices. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

1025 ET - The Bank of England will probably refrain from cutting interest rates further this year but sterling could weaken as this is priced in and U.K. fiscal sustainability concerns are building, Rabobank's Jane Foley says in a note. Markets remain squarely focused on the U.K.'s difficult budget position, she says. Friday's worse-than-expected U.K. public sector borrowing data for August provided a "timely reminder" of this and the challenges that await Treasury chief Rachel Reeves as she prepares for November's autumn budget, she says. Rabobank expects the euro to rise to 0.88 in six months. It last trades up 0.2% at 0.8714 pounds, having earlier reached a six-week high of 0.8728, LSEG data show. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

1022 ET - Gold prices extend gains in afternoon trade, with futures rising 0.4% to $3,693.60 a troy ounce. The precious metal is up 0.2% this week as investors interpreted the Federal Reserve's tone on future policy as less dovish than expected. However, it is still up nearly 10% this month on U.S. rate-cut optimism, safe-haven demand and central-bank buying. "We assume that the momentum has run out for now, even though China's gold imports from Hong Kong next week could show that Asian gold buyers are gradually 'getting used' to the higher price level," Barbara Lambrecht at Commerzbank says. "However, as we expect more interest-rate cuts by the Fed next year than the market does, we anticipate even higher prices on the gold market in the long term." (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0955 ET - Statistics Canada's estimate retail sales rebounded in August from weakness the month before backs expectations Canada's economy picked up after a 2Q contraction. Capital Economics' Thomas Ryan says the estimate sales rose 1% on-month implies a 0.8% rebound in sales volumes, given higher goods prices. He says that would suggest sales volumes are on course to rise 1.2% annualized in 3Q, consistent with a moderate slowdown in consumption growth to about 2% from 4.5%. "Given falling employment and a rising jobless rate, that wouldn't be considered a bad result," Ryan says, adding it tracks with expectations the economy will grow in the remainder of the year. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

0951 ET - Canadian July retail sales were soft, but a decent August suggests Canadian consumers didn't stay down for long, Bank of Montreal's Shelly Kaushik says. Sales fell 0.8% on-month in July, with the auto sector the one positive. Still, the flash estimate for August indicates a 1% rise in sales. "Despite ongoing trade uncertainty and further weakening in the labor market, the economy looks to be on track for a modest recovery to start the third quarter," Kaushik says. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbMStewart)

0939 ET - Only five of the Fed's 12 reserve-bank presidents vote on policy each year, and in 2025, the Cleveland Fed's Beth Hammack is not a voter. But the reserve banks submit input on how the Fed should adjust its discount lending rate, which typically moves in concert with its main benchmark interest-rate target. At Wednesday's meeting, the Cleveland Fed was one of the only reserve banks not to request that the discount rate be lowered alongside the fed funds target range. Bill Nelson of the Bank Policy Institute suggests this can be read as a "shadow dissent" from Hammack, who has been hawkish. "In this case, I infer that President Hammack would have preferred no change to the FOMC target," he writes. Hammack will be a voter again starting in January. (matt.grossman@wsj.com; @mattgrossman)

0928 ET - Fed independence has become an even hotter topic with Governor Miran still holding his job at the White House while voting for an interest rate cut deeper than his FOMC colleagues supported. But Wall Street seems to have taken the change in stride, which Minneapolis Fed's Neel Kashkari attributes to confidence in U.S. institutions. "There's widespread appreciation for how important Fed independence is," among investors and lawmakers, he says on CNBC. "I think people are betting on the institutions of the country continuing to keep Fed independence, to keep it outside of the short-term political process." (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0927 ET - The dollar has scope for a short-term recovery as the Federal Reserve is unlikely to cut interest rates as aggressively as what markets expect, Danske Bank analyst Mohamad Al-Saraf says in a note. "While markets are pricing sequential cuts in October and December, we believe the data favors a more gradual approach and expect the Fed to cut again in December." However, the dollar is still on course to weaken over the longer run, he says. Danske Bank expects the euro to rise to $1.23 in 12 months, supported by favorable rate differentials, a recovering European market, investors hedging against a weaker dollar and diminished confidence in U.S. institutions. The euro falls 0.3% to $1.1757.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0913 ET - Credit spreads on euro-denominated credit remains tight, reflecting the market's optimism around eurozone and global economic growth, LBBW credit analysts say in a note. "If the macro data fails to meet the high expectations in the coming months, there is a risk of disappointment on the market for risk assets which might lead to an increase in credit spreads," they say. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0911 ET - Tariffs make the Fed's job harder. "We are having to adjust policy to try to accommodate and keep the labor markets strong at the same time," Minneapolis Fed's Neel Kashkari says on CNBC. He says underlying inflation components such as housing services and wages are cooling. "I've got pretty good confidence that those two segments are going to continue to come down." Kashkari says core goods inflation was declining before the tariffs and he is getting more convinced the levies represent a one-off impact on prices. "But it's going to take a couple of years for it to play out." (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0904 ET - Keeping its credibility seems to be more of a concern for the Fed after it cut interest rates while inflation remains above target. "We need to work extra hard to explain to the public why we are making the actions that we are making," Minneapolis Fed's Neel Kashkari says on CNBC. "Why we are cutting rates right now, in light of the elevated inflation that we've all been experiencing for four years," he says. "We're not okay with 3% inflation." Kashkari says weakness in labor markets support lower rates while inflation tends to moderate. "I'm confident that we are going to get inflation back down to 2%." (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

0850 ET - Longer-term Treasury yields rise faster than shorter-term ones as markets hear from Fed officials what went on in this week's pivotal decision to cut interest rates. Minneapolis Fed's Neel Kashkari publishes an essay saying the labor market "appears to be weakening" and "nominal wage growth continues to moderate." He adds that inflation remains high but inflation expectations haven't risen as expected. "I do not believe we should be on a preset course for a series of rate cuts," Kashkari writes. The 10-year yield is at 4.133% and the two-year at 3.580%. The WSJ Dollar Index is up 0.4%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

data.title: TSX hits another record high, set for seven straight weekly gains
data.description:
By Twesha Dikshit

Canada's main stock index continued its record-setting run on Friday, set to extend its string of weekly gains two days after interest rate cuts by the U.S. and Canadian central banks.

Toronto's S&P/TSX composite index added 0.5% to 29,602.19 points by 10:07 ET (1407 GMT), after hitting a record intra-day high earlier in the session. The index was set for a seventh-consecutive week of gains, its longest such streak in over a year.

The BoC reduced its key policy rate to a three-year low of 2.5% on Wednesday, saying it was prepared for further cuts if risks to the economy rose.

Retail sales data from July showed a 0.8% drop as widely anticipated, but an advanced indicator pointed to retail sales growth rebounding to 1% in August.

"Canadian retail sales were disappointing which has continued a series of negative economic numbers for Canada, and is going to keep the pressure on the Bank of Canada for cutting rates," said Colin Cieszynski, chief market strategist at SIA Wealth Management.

Gold mining stocks (.SPTTGD) led gains on the day, rising 3.1%, tracking bullion prices.

Barrick Gold Mining rose 6.3% while Kinross Gold and Endeavour Mining added over 3% each.

Energy stocks were the laggards on the day, dropping 1.7% while most indexes were in the green.

Among other stocks, mining company Orla Mining dropped 7.1% to $14.18 after gold miner Newmont said it sold its entire stake for $439 million.

Toronto Stock Exchange's index currently trades at a 12-month forward price-earning ratio of 16.45, its highest level since May 2021, according to data from LSEG Datastream.

"Valuations have certainly gotten stretched. This isn't an issue specific to Canada. It's more a case of the trend is your friend till it ends," Cieszynski said.

data.title: REG - Neo Energy Metals - Update on Audit Progress and Trading Resumption
data.description:
RNS Number : 1160A Neo Energy Metals PLC 19 September 2025  

Neo Energy Metals plc / LSE: NEO, A2X: NEO / Market: Main Market of the London Stock Exchange

19 September 2025

Neo Energy Metals plc

('Neo Energy' or 'the Company')

Update on Audit Progress and Trading Resumption

Neo Energy, the near-term, low-cost uranium developer, provides a comprehensive update on the status of its annual report and accounts for the year ended 30 September 2024 (the "Accounts"), regulatory timelines, and strategic initiatives.

Further to the Company's announcement on 3 September 2025, the Company confirms that it continues to make substantive progress towards the completion of its annual audit and accounts. The Company remains in active and constructive dialogue with its auditors and the relevant regulatory bodies.

Work on the audit is ongoing, with the accounts of key operating subsidiaries in South Africa expected to be finalised and signed off next week. The UK audit process is continuing in parallel and is also expected to be concluded in the near term.

The Board is focused on resolving all outstanding administrative matters and remains committed to the resumption of trading of its ordinary shares on the London Stock Exchange at the earliest opportunity.

Operational Progress

Alongside audit and listing preparations, the Company continues to progress the regulatory approval processes for the projects acquired in 2024. These efforts remain a strategic priority and form the foundation for near-term development activity.

Investor Presentation

Mr Jason Brewer, Neo's Chairman, will be in London during October and anticipates hosting an Investor Presentation on Tuesday, 14 October 2025. Further details on the time and location will be advised in due course.

The Company extends gratitude to its shareholders for their continued patience and support. Further updates will be provided as the Company advances through the final stages of this process.

ENDS

About NEO Energy Metals Plc

Neo Energy Metals plc is a uranium developer and mining company listed on the main market of the London Stock Exchange .

The Company and its South African subsidiaries, namely Neo Uranium Resources Beisa Mine (Pty) Limited and Neo Uranium Resources South Africa (Pty) Ltd, have continued to strengthen the uranium portfolio through conditional agreements for the acquisitions of 100% interest in the Beisa North and Beisa South Uranium and Gold Projects and 100% interest in the Beatrix 4 mine and shaft complex, the processing plant complex and associated infrastructure in the Witwatersrand Basin, located in the Free State Province of South Africa. The combined projects' total SAMREC Code compliant resource base comprises 117 million pounds of U₃O₈ and over 5 million ounces of gold.

Additionally, the Company holds up to a 70% stake in the Henkries Uranium Project, an advanced, low-cost mine located in South Africa's Northern Cape Province and a 100% interest in the Henkries South Uranium Project, extending the Henkries Project's strike length by 10km to a total of 46km of shallow paleo-channels proven to host uranium mineralisation through extensive drilling and feasibility studies backed by US$30 million in historic exploration and development expenditure.

The Company is led by a proven board and management team with experience in uranium and mineral project development in Southern Africa. Neo Energy's strategy focuses on an accelerated development and production approach to generate cash flow from Henkries while planning for long-term exploration and portfolio growth in the highly prospective uranium district of Africa.

The Company's shares are also listed on the A2X Markets (A2X: NEO), an independent South African stock exchange, to expand its investor base and facilitate strategic acquisitions of uranium projects, particularly within South Africa.
